title: viola montana subsp. schultzii
intro: Viola montana subsp. schultzii, also known as the alpine pansy, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the viola genus and is native to the European Alps. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, and its extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Viola montana subsp. schultzii is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
